notes: RCT (n=11): 3g glycine before bed improved subjective sleep quality, reduced sleep latency, and improved next-day alertness in partially sleep-restricted volunteers; small sample but placebo-controlled
Mechanistic study confirming glycine's sleep effects are mediated via NMDA receptors in the SCN; peripheral vasodilation lowers core body temperature to initiate sleep; corroborates RCT mechanism

2024 review on glycine supplementation for physical performance and recovery; covers collagen synthesis role, endogenous synthesis gap (~3g/day produced vs ~10-15g needed), and connective tissue applications
Comprehensive synthesis of glycine evidence across sleep, cognition, metabolic health, and collagen; regularly updated; confirms moderate overall evidence quality and 3g pre-sleep dosing
